title: rubus foliosus subsp. flexuosus
intro: Rubus foliosus subsp. flexuosus is a subspecies of the Rubus foliosus plant, commonly known as the flexible leaf raspberry or the wavy-leaved raspberry, which is a type of bramble native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The leaves and stems of Rubus foliosus subsp. flexuosus have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Rubus foliosus subsp. flexuosus include its ability to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as arthritis, gout, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to support immune function and reduce oxidative stress. Rubus foliosus subsp. flexuosus can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by drying and processing the leaves and stems of the plant.
